title: Ductwork Insulation

internal_lining.name: Internal Acoustic/Thermal Lining
internal_lining.function: Acoustic attenuation of airborne noise within ductwork AND thermal insulation. Dual purpose.

glass_wool_mat.name: Glass Wool Mat (Flexible)
glass_wool_mat.description: Flexible mat bonded to internal duct surface with adhesive and mechanical fixings (pins).
glass_wool_mat.standard: BS EN 13162 (mineral wool thermal insulation products)
glass_wool_mat.facing: Neoprene-coated glass tissue facing — erosion-resistant to airflow up to 20 m/s.
glass_wool_mat.note: Most common for standard commercial ductwork.

rigid_slab.name: Rigid Acoustic Slab
rigid_slab.description: Dense rigid mineral wool slab for higher acoustic performance.
rigid_slab.use: Where enhanced acoustic attenuation required — near plant rooms, above sensitive areas.
rigid_slab.note: Heavier and more expensive than flexible mat. Better at low-frequency noise.

closed_cell_foam.name: Closed-Cell Foam Lining
closed_cell_foam.description: Elastomeric or PU foam sheet bonded internally. Smoother surface, easier to clean.
closed_cell_foam.use: Clean rooms, laboratories, healthcare — where hygiene is important.
closed_cell_foam.note: Higher cost but smoother, non-fibrous internal surface. No fibre shedding risk.

thicknesses.25mm: Standard thickness for general acoustic/thermal lining.
thicknesses.50mm: Enhanced acoustic performance. Used near plant, above meeting rooms, in noise-sensitive areas.
thicknesses.note: Thickness affects INTERNAL duct dimensions — a duct lined with 25mm each side loses 50mm total from both W and H. Designer should have accounted for this in duct sizing.

measurement.formula_rectangular: 2 × (W_internal + H_internal) × Length. Use INTERNAL dimensions (duct size minus 2× lining thickness each side).
measurement.note: Internal lining is measured by the INTERNAL surface it covers, not the external duct dimensions.

duct_sizing_impact.rule: Internal lining REDUCES the effective duct cross-section. A 600×400 duct with 25mm lining has effective airway of 550×350.
duct_sizing_impact.flag_if: Internal lining specified but duct sizes appear to be NOMINAL (not enlarged to compensate). Designer may have sized duct before adding lining thickness — flag for design check.
duct_sizing_impact.note: The designer should size ducts to maintain required airflow AFTER lining is installed. If unsure, flag.

fixing.method: Adhesive + insulation pins (weld pins or stick pins) with speed washers at ~300mm centres.
fixing.accessories: Insulation pins, speed washers, adhesive, joint tape.
fixing.note: Pins are welded to duct surface (before installation) or self-adhesive stick pins (applied after duct installation).

external_wrap.name: External Thermal Wrap / Board
external_wrap.function: Thermal insulation applied to the OUTSIDE of ductwork. Prevents heat loss (hot ducts) or condensation (cold ducts).

glass_wool_roll.name: Glass Wool Roll (Flexible Wrap)
glass_wool_roll.description: Flexible roll of glass wool, typically foil-faced. Wrapped around duct exterior.
glass_wool_roll.facing: Aluminium foil laminate — standard. Provides vapour resistance and clean finish.
glass_wool_roll.note: Most common and cost-effective for standard HVAC ducts.

mineral_wool_slab.name: Mineral Wool Rigid Slab
mineral_wool_slab.description: Rigid mineral wool slab wired or banded to duct exterior. For larger/heavier ducts.
mineral_wool_slab.use: External ductwork, plant rooms, where more robust insulation needed.
mineral_wool_slab.note: More labour-intensive than roll wrap. Better for flat surfaces (rectangular duct).

elastomeric_sheet.name: Elastomeric Foam Sheet
elastomeric_sheet.description: Closed-cell rubber sheet (Armaflex/K-Flex) applied externally to duct.
elastomeric_sheet.use: Cold air ducts, chilled water AHU supply, where condensation prevention is critical.
elastomeric_sheet.vapour_barrier: Inherent — closed-cell structure provides integral vapour barrier.
elastomeric_sheet.note: Essential for COLD air ductwork (supply air from chilled coil). Glass wool is NOT suitable for cold duct without additional vapour barrier.

phenolic_slab.name: Phenolic Foam Slab
phenolic_slab.description: Rigid phenolic board applied externally. Thinnest profile for thermal performance.
phenolic_slab.use: Where space is very limited and thermal performance must be maximised.
phenolic_slab.note: More expensive than glass wool. Justified in tight ceiling voids.

thicknesses.25mm: Standard for supply air ductwork, general HVAC.
thicknesses.50mm: Enhanced thermal performance. External ductwork, high temperature differential, Part L compliance.
thicknesses.75mm: High-performance. Hot duct in cold space, or where Part L requires enhanced insulation.
thicknesses.note: Check BS 5422 Table 12 for minimum thicknesses by duct temperature and ambient conditions.

measurement.formula_rectangular: 2 × (W_external + H_external) × Length. Use EXTERNAL duct dimensions.
measurement.note: External wrap is measured by the OUTSIDE surface of the duct (duct fabrication dimensions).

vapour_barrier.rule: Required on ALL cold duct external insulation (supply air from cooling coil, chilled air distribution).

types.foil_facing: Aluminium foil laminate on insulation roll — provides adequate VB for most applications. Joints must be sealed with foil tape.
types.separate_membrane: Additional polythene or foil membrane over insulation — for enhanced VB performance.
types.elastomeric: Inherent VB in closed-cell material — no additional VB needed.

vapour_barrier.joint_sealing: ALL joints in vapour barrier MUST be sealed with foil tape or vapour barrier tape. Unsealed joints allow moisture ingress → condensation → insulation failure.
vapour_barrier.flag_if: Cold duct insulation specified without vapour barrier — flag as condensation risk.

fixing.roll_wrap: Self-adhesive lap, or pinned with insulation pins + speed washers + foil tape on joints.
fixing.slab: Wired with galvanised wire, or banded with stainless steel banding at ~450mm centres.
fixing.accessories: Foil tape, insulation pins, speed washers, wire ties, banding clips.

fire_rated_wrap.name: Fire-Rated Duct Wrap / Fire Protection
fire_rated_wrap.function: Maintains fire compartmentation where ductwork passes through fire-rated walls or floors WITHOUT a fire damper.
fire_rated_wrap.standard: BS EN 1366-1 (fire resistance tests for ducts)

mineral_wool_wrap.name: Mineral Wool Fire Wrap
mineral_wool_wrap.description: Dense mineral wool mat or slab, typically 60-100kg/m³ density, wrapped around duct and secured with pins/wire.
mineral_wool_wrap.brands: Rockwool Firestop, Isover ULTIMATE, Knauf Fire Roll
mineral_wool_wrap.note: Must be tested and certified to BS EN 1366-1 for the specific fire rating. Not all mineral wool achieves fire rating — only tested systems.

intumescent_wrap.name: Intumescent Wrap
intumescent_wrap.description: Thin intumescent sheet that expands when exposed to fire, providing insulation barrier.
intumescent_wrap.use: Where space is limited for thick mineral wool fire wrap.
intumescent_wrap.note: Thinner profile than mineral wool fire wrap but more expensive.

fire_rated_board.name: Fire-Rated Board System
fire_rated_board.description: Calcium silicate or vermiculite board enclosure around duct.
fire_rated_board.use: Large ducts, risers, where board enclosure is more practical than wrapping.
fire_rated_board.note: Creates a fire-rated enclosure. May be builder's work rather than insulation scope. Check.

fire_ratings.typical_use: Major fire walls, stair enclosures, protected shafts

measurement.unit: m² (duct surface area covered by fire wrap)
measurement.scope: Fire-rated wrap must extend: from the fire-rated wall face to a distance specified in the test certificate (typically 500mm-1500mm either side of the wall). Check manufacturer certification.
measurement.note: Do NOT assume fire wrap covers the entire duct run — it only covers the section through and either side of the fire barrier.

when_required: Ductwork passing through fire-rated walls WITHOUT a fire damper installed.
Ductwork within a protected escape route (stair enclosure, protected corridor).
Ductwork connecting to a fire-rated riser or shaft.
Kitchen extract ductwork through fire compartments (DW/172 + fire wrap).
Smoke extract ductwork (must maintain integrity during fire — BS EN 12101).

fire_damper_vs_fire_wrap.rule: At a fire-rated penetration, EITHER a fire damper OR fire-rated duct wrap is used — not both.
fire_damper_vs_fire_wrap.fire_damper: Fire damper = mechanical closure device inside the duct at the wall penetration. Stops airflow. Standard approach.
fire_damper_vs_fire_wrap.fire_wrap: Fire wrap = insulation around the duct for a distance either side of the wall. Duct remains open. Used where fire dampers cannot be used (e.g. smoke extract, kitchen extract).
fire_damper_vs_fire_wrap.both_wrong: If BOTH fire damper AND fire wrap are shown at the same penetration, flag for design clarification — this is unusual.
fire_damper_vs_fire_wrap.note: Fire dampers are covered in KB-M03. Fire wrap is covered here in KB-I02.

flag_triggers: Fire rating requirement stated without specific product/thickness — flag.
Fire wrap shown on drawing without fire rating noted — flag.
Duct penetrating fire wall with no fire damper AND no fire wrap — flag as non-compliant.
Fire wrap specified without reference to tested system (BS EN 1366-1 cert) — flag.
Kitchen extract duct through fire wall without DW/172 + fire wrap — flag.

surface_area.rule: Ductwork insulation is measured in m² of duct surface area. NEVER measure duct insulation in linear metres (except circular duct where diameter is stated).

rectangular.variables: W = width (m), H = height (m), L = length (m)

example.note: Use external dimensions for external wrap. Use internal dimensions for internal lining.
Use external diameter for external wrap.

circular.alternative: Circular duct insulation can also be measured in m (linear) if diameter is always stated alongside. Check pricing method — some price per m, others per m².

flat_oval.variables: W = major axis (m), H = minor axis (m), L = length (m)

practical_notes: Include duct surface area at bends, tees, and fittings — these are insulated too.
Fittings allowance: add 10-15% to straight duct surface area to cover insulation at fittings.
If fittings are individually drawn, calculate their surface area separately for accuracy.
Access doors in insulated ductwork need insulated access panels — count as nr items.
Insulation stops at: terminal devices (diffusers, grilles), equipment connections (AHU, FCU), fire dampers (unless fire wrap continues through).

internal_vs_external.rule: ALWAYS confirm whether spec requires INTERNAL lining, EXTERNAL wrap, or BOTH.
internal_vs_external.both: Some specs require BOTH internal lining (for acoustic) AND external wrap (for thermal). These are SEPARATE items with SEPARATE measurements.
internal_vs_external.flag_if: Spec mentions "insulated ductwork" without stating internal, external, or both — flag for clarification.

supply_air.typical: External thermal wrap — prevents heat gain/loss between AHU and room.
supply_air.cold_supply: If supply air is cooled (from chilled water coil), vapour barrier is ESSENTIAL on external wrap.
supply_air.note: Supply air ducts are almost always insulated (thermal + energy efficiency). Uninsulated supply duct = energy waste.

return_air.typical: Often NOT insulated if duct is within the conditioned space (ceiling void).
return_air.exceptions: Insulate return duct if: in unconditioned space (external, plant room), long return run with significant heat gain/loss, spec requires it.
return_air.note: Check spec — many projects do not insulate return air ducts. Flag if spec is silent on return duct insulation.

extract_air.typical: Usually NOT insulated — extract air is being discarded.
extract_air.exceptions: Insulate if: kitchen extract (DW/172 fire wrap may be needed), fume extract (chemical containment), or where duct is in cold space and condensation is a risk.
extract_air.note: Toilet extract, general extract — typically uninsulated unless spec states otherwise.

fresh_air_intake.typical: External wrap with vapour barrier — cold outside air will cause condensation on duct surface.
fresh_air_intake.note: Fresh air intake duct is COLD — always insulate with vapour barrier. Often missed in specs. Flag if not specified.

kitchen_extract.typical: Fire-rated wrap — DW/172 spec ductwork. Fire wrap through compartment walls.
kitchen_extract.note: Kitchen extract may need fire wrap for full length in some fire strategies, not just at wall penetrations. Check fire strategy.

smoke_extract.typical: Fire-rated wrap or fire-rated duct system — must maintain integrity during fire.
smoke_extract.standard: BS EN 12101 for smoke control systems.
smoke_extract.note: Smoke extract ductwork fire rating is determined by the fire engineer. Check fire strategy document.

flexible_duct.rule: Flexible ductwork is often supplied PRE-INSULATED (insulated flex). Check spec.
flexible_duct.pre_insulated: If flex is pre-insulated, do NOT add separate insulation — it is included in the flex duct item.
flexible_duct.uninsulated: If flex is uninsulated (bare metallic flex), separate insulation may be required — check spec.
flexible_duct.flag_if: Spec does not state whether flexible duct is insulated or uninsulated type — flag.

flag_triggers: Fire rating requirement stated without specific product, thickness, or tested system reference — flag.
Ductwork shown on drawing without insulation specification where insulation would normally be expected (supply air, fresh air intake) — flag.
Internal lining specified without stated effect on duct sizing — flag for design check.
Spec requiring vapour barrier without specifying material type or sealing method — flag.
Cold duct (chilled supply, fresh air intake) specified with mineral wool wrap but no vapour barrier — flag condensation risk.
Fire wrap extent not stated (how far either side of wall) — flag. Must match tested system certificate.
Duct penetrating fire wall with neither fire damper nor fire wrap — flag as non-compliant.
Kitchen extract duct through fire compartment without fire wrap reference — flag.
Internal AND external insulation both specified — confirm both are intended (not a spec error).
Return air duct insulation not mentioned — clarify if intentionally uninsulated or spec omission.
Flexible duct insulation status not stated (pre-insulated or bare) — flag.
Insulation shown stopping at a point on drawing with no apparent reason — flag for clarification.
Aluminium cladding over duct insulation specified — count as separate item (m²). Not included in basic insulation rate.
Insulation to existing ductwork specified — flag. May need removal of old insulation first (asbestos risk if pre-2000).
